select_when: A person or group is defending a prior commitment, identity, or promise more strongly than the evidence now justifies.
Escalation, rigidity, or self-justification is blocking reversal even though the original assumptions have weakened.
The real issue is not lack of information but the social or psychological cost of admitting the earlier choice may have been wrong.
The team needs to inspect how small public commitments are shaping larger downstream behavior.
avoid_when: The situation mainly needs healthy follow-through or purpose alignment rather than a bias audit on rigid commitment.
The model is being used to mock consistency itself instead of distinguishing disciplined persistence from self-deceptive lock-in.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model on self-justification, rigidity, and social-consistency pressure rather than broad influence-principle enthusiasm.
donor_drops: Did not widen into generic commitment or culture rhetoric because the raw source is strongest on rationalization, group loyalty, and reversal difficulty.
open_questions: The source mixes productive consistency with pathological rigidity, so the avoid conditions work hard to keep the curated use narrow.

select_when.note: The source is explicit on self-justification, group loyalty, bias blind spots, and small commitments shaping larger behavior.

avoid_when.note: The avoid lines are conservative compression of the source's distinction between useful consistency and harmful rigidity.

input_type.note: The source activates around escalating commitment and rationalization; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields reversal and de-biasing checks rather than a general communication action; the output label reflects that tighter use.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses rigid commitment and requires metacognitive inspection of self-justifying stories.
